title: Use coverage gaps to auto-propose new Elyos good-deed projects
context: Backlog: ATLTUAE as a meta-tool. Its coverage ledger's persistent gaps are signals of where the knowledge commons most needs work, which can seed new Elyos good-deed projects. This research task mines coverage gaps to auto-propose candidate deeds for human review.
objective: Build a method that reads the coverage ledger's persistent gaps and auto-proposes candidate new Elyos good-deed projects (as human-reviewed proposals), turning the engine's map of its own ignorance into a deed-seeding signal.
acceptanceCriteria: Mines the coverage ledger for persistent/high-value gaps and proposes candidate good-deed projects with rationale
Proposals are human-reviewed before any project is created (no auto-creation)
Proposals respect Elyos refusal guardrails (no malware/surveillance/disinformation/for-profit-primary deeds)
